chief_complaint: A 28-year-old female was admitted with low oxygen saturation despite oxygen supplementation.
history: A previously healthy female was admitted following inpatient antibiotic treatment for a lower respiratory tract infection. Since admission, her pulse oximetry revealed low oxygen saturation despite oxygen supplementation. Suspicion of methaemoglobinaemia was raised.
exam: Vital signs: pulse oximetry revealed low oxygen saturation, oxygen pressure in arterial blood remained normal.
labs: Methaemoglobinaemia reduction test (Brewer's test) became positive and was confirmed with a blood methaemoglobin assay value of 18.19%. High-performance liquid chromatography excluded the presence of haemoglobin-M.
correct_diagnosis: Methaemoglobinaemia as a rare complication of Mycoplasma pneumoniae pneumonia
differentials: Methaemoglobinaemia as a rare complication of Mycoplasma pneumoniae pneumonia
correct_ranking: Methaemoglobinaemia as a rare complication of Mycoplasma pneumoniae pneumonia
